compliance_framework.description: Scale-Based Regulation (SBR) compliance guidelines and requirements for Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs).

ICC.name: Investment and Credit Company
ICC.description: Any company which is a financial institution carrying on as its principal business- asset finance, providing finance whether by making loans or advances or otherwise for any activity other than its own and the acquisition of securities.

Type-I.name: Type-I NBFC
Type-I.description: NBFCs which do not accept/hold public funds and do not have customer interface.

MFI.name: Micro Finance Institution
MFI.description: Non-deposit taking NBFC having not less than 85% of its assets in the nature of qualifying assets, which satisfy the criteria laid down by RBI.

HFC.name: Housing Finance Company
HFC.description: A financial institution carrying on as its principal business the providing of finance, whether directly or indirectly, for acquisition or construction of houses.

CIC.name: Core Investment Company
CIC.description: Non-Banking Financial Company carrying on the business of acquisition of shares and securities.

P2P.name: Peer-to-Peer Lending Platform
P2P.description: An intermediary providing the services of a peer-to-peer lending platform.

AA.name: Account Aggregator
AA.description: An entity retrieving or collecting financial information pertaining to a customer and consolidating it.

Factor.name: NBFC-Factor
Factor.description: Non-deposit taking NBFC carrying on factoring business as its principal business.

IFC.name: Infrastructure Finance Company
IFC.description: Non-deposit taking NBFC which deploys at least 74% of its total assets in infrastructure loans.

PD.name: Primary Dealer
PD.description: Standalone Primary Dealers (SPDs) registered with RBI to deal in government securities.

IDF.name: Infrastructure Debt Fund
IDF.description: A non-deposit taking NBFC that facilitates the flow of long-term debt into infrastructure projects.

NOFHC.name: Non-Operative Financial Holding Company
NOFHC.description: Financial holding company registered as an NBFC through which promoter / promoter groups will be permitted to set up a new bank.

MGC.name: Mortgage Guarantee Company
MGC.description: Company where at least 90% of business turnover is mortgage guarantee business or at least 90% of gross income is from mortgage guarantee business.

Base.description: Consists of non-deposit taking NBFCs below the asset size of ₹1000 crore, and specific categories like NBFC-P2P, NBFC-AA, NOFHC, and Type-I NBFCs.

Middle.description: Consists of deposit-taking NBFCs (irrespective of asset size), non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹1000 crore and above, and special categories like HFC, IFC, IDF, CIC, and SPDs.

Upper.description: Consists of those NBFCs which are specifically identified by the RBI as warranting enhanced regulatory requirement based on a set of parameters and scoring methodology.

Top.description: This layer will remain empty unless the RBI is of the opinion that there is a significant increase in the systemic risk of a specific NBFC in the Upper Layer, moving it to the Top Layer.

monthly.name: Short-term Dynamic Liquidity Statement
monthly.applicability: NBFCs with asset size >= ₹100 crore or accepting deposits

quarterly.name: Structural Liquidity & Interest Rate Sensitivity
quarterly.applicability: NBFCs with asset size >= ₹100 crore or accepting deposits

annual.name: Active / Non-Deposit Taking Return (Capital Adequacy & NOF)
annual.applicability: Base Layer NBFCs with asset size < ₹100 crore

fair_practice_code.description: A set of guidelines that NBFCs must follow in their dealings with customers, ensuring transparency and ethical lending.

grievance_redressal.ombudsman_applicable: For NBFCs with customer interface and asset size >= ₹100 crore
